title: bud.bats: use absolute paths in newly-added tests
body:
Gating tests run in a temp directory; relative paths (bud/foo/bar)
will pass in CI but cause gating-test failures.

Solution is trivial: always use `${TESTSDIR}/bud/etc`

Signed-off-by: Ed Santiago <santiago@redhat.com>

#### What type of PR is this?

/kind failing-test 

#### What this PR does / why we need it:

Fedora gating tests are failing on rawhide with errors like:
```
$ /usr/bin/buildah bud --timestamp=0 --quiet --pull=false --signature-policy /usr/share/buildah/test/system/policy.json -t timestamp -f Dockerfile.1 bud/cache-stages
error evaluating symlinks in build context path: lstat /var/str/bud: no such file or directory
[ rc=125 (** EXPECTED 0 **) ]
```

#### How to verify it

Run `bats /path/to/bud.bats` while cd'ed to a directory other than the git checkout

#### Which issue(s) this PR fixes:

None

#### Special notes for your reviewer:

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

None
